commit.message:
Update larvling/scripts/preflight.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(dashboard): copy agent to chain + skill diff order fix

- New '📋 Copy to Chain…' button on agent detail page
  - Modal lists only eligible chains (excludes source + chains where the
    agent already has thoughts) to prevent diverged histories
  - User must type the target chain name to confirm before copying
  - POST /dashboard/api/agents/{chain}/{agent}/copy-to/{target}
  - 409 Conflict if agent already present on target chain
  - Semantic fields preserved; positional refs/relations intentionally dropped
  - Agent display_name + owner propagated via first appended thought

- Fix skill diff version picker default order
  - From selector: chronological order, v1 pre-selected
  - To selector: chronological order, last version pre-selected
  - Previously From was reversed and To defaulted to currently viewed version

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: mentisdb
repository.description: Memory that lasts and compounds. MentisDB gives agents durable memory so they do not just remember, they improve over time. It stores append-only thought chains   plus a Git-like skills registry, letting skills evolve with experience through versioned, integrity-checked updates and fast retrieval.

commit.message:
fix: improve print preview and printing quality

- Move shadow from individual pages to preview wrapper to eliminate borders between pages
- Add comprehensive @media print rules for proper page breaks and clean print output
- Replace flexbox footer positioning with absolute positioning for reliable bottom placement
- Add min-height: 44px to buttons for consistency with invoice generator
- Remove unnecessary print-page-body wrapper and flexbox layout from PRINT_CSS
- Add position: relative to print pages for absolute footer positioning

Fixes issues where:
- Shadow appeared as borders between pages in preview
- Shadow sometimes showed in printed output
- Footer was not always at bottom of page
- Button heights were inconsistent across apps

tables, and blockquote callouts left unchanged.

Converted all 62 standalone bullet-point lines (lines starting with
'- ' or '  - ') outside blockquotes into integrated prose paragraphs,
targeting the following sections:

- Laboratory monitoring bullets (assessment section)

All clinical facts preserved; no content added or removed.

commit.message:
deprecate(config): rename MENTISDB_DEFAULT_KEY → MENTISDB_DEFAULT_CHAIN_KEY

- New canonical env var: MENTISDB_DEFAULT_CHAIN_KEY
- Old name MENTISDB_DEFAULT_KEY accepted as a backward-compatible alias
  via env_var(&["MENTISDB_DEFAULT_CHAIN_KEY", "MENTISDB_DEFAULT_KEY"])
- Updated: server.rs rustdoc, mentisdbd.rs module doc + startup print,
  .env.example, start_mentisdbd, README.md, changelog.txt (0.5.1.1)

commit.message:
feat(dashboard): agent memory export — Export Memory dialog with Copy + Save As

- New GET /dashboard/api/agents/{chain_key}/{agent_id}/memory-markdown endpoint
  calls chain.to_memory_markdown() filtered to the agent; returns markdown + filename
- Agent detail page: '📄 Export Memory…' button opens a full-screen dialog
  with chain dropdown (pre-populated with all chains the agent appears in),
  Generate button, scrollable textarea, ⎘ Copy and ⬇ Save As… buttons
- Save As triggers a browser Blob download as <agent>_<chain>_AGENT.md
- Copy falls back to execCommand for older browsers
- ThoughtQuery added to dashboard.rs imports

Also includes:
- chore: bump Cargo.toml version to 0.5.1
- chore: update changelog.txt with 0.5.1.0 entry
- docs(lib): ThoughtQuery refactor + rich rustdoc pass from doc agent
- docs(bench): enriched bench comments from doc agent

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: mentisdb
repository.description: Memory that lasts and compounds. MentisDB gives agents durable memory so they do not just remember, they improve over time. It stores append-only thought chains   plus a Git-like skills registry, letting skills evolve with experience through versioned, integrity-checked updates and fast retrieval.

commit.message:
docs(skills): world-class rustdoc pass — SkillRegistry, SkillUpload, search, revoke, deprecate examples

- SkillFormat: rich type-level doc explaining format round-trips; runnable Display/FromStr
  example showing 'md' alias and error on unknown format
- SkillStatus: full lifecycle table (Active/Deprecated/Revoked), semantic variant docs,
  runnable roundtrip example with 'disabled' alias
- SkillSection: Markdown section-model explanation with runnable parse example
- SkillDocument: frontmatter-field table, Markdown body-parsing prose, runnable
  import→JSON round-trip example (uses ## headings to avoid rustdoc hidden-line stripping)
- SkillUpload<'a>: builder table, comprehensive example showing all three optional
  builder methods (with_skill_id, with_agent_identity, with_signing)
- SkillQuery: field-semantics table (AND across fields, OR within), builder-style
  example with text/tags/statuses/formats/limit filters
- SkillRegistry: immutable-versioned-history design explanation, Full vs Delta storage
  prose, lifecycle section, persistence/index section, complete no_run example
  (open → upload → list → search → read)
- upload_skill: version-storage semantics, status-behaviour note, no_run two-upload
  example confirming version_count and skill_versions
- list_skills: ordering note, no_run example showing updated_at ordering and
  status/version_count fields
- search_skills: two-phase filter explanation (indexed pass + linear scan), no_run
  example with tag, text, and limit filters
- skill_document vs read_skill: comparison table, no_run examples for each,
  cross-format (upload Markdown, read JSON) demo
- deprecate_skill: semantic distinction from revoke, auto-Active-restore note,
  no_run example showing status/reason/version_count preserved
- revoke_skill: safety-signal semantics, Revoked-not-cleared-by-upload behaviour,
  no_run example showing subsequent upload still stays Revoked
- import_skill: round-trip prose (Markdown→JSON→Markdown), runnable example with
  frontmatter + sections assertion + cross-format re-export
- export_skill: programmatic SkillDocument construction example, Markdown/JSON
  output assertions, round-trip through Markdown
- Fix: all examples use ## headings inside raw-string literals to avoid the
  rustdoc hidden-line stripping bug (lines starting with '# ' get '# ' stripped)
- All 20 skills.rs doctests green; pre-existing server.rs failures untouched

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: mentisdb
repository.description: Memory that lasts and compounds. MentisDB gives agents durable memory so they do not just remember, they improve over time. It stores append-only thought chains   plus a Git-like skills registry, letting skills evolve with experience through versioned, integrity-checked updates and fast retrieval.

commit.message:
docs(server): world-class rustdoc pass — MentisDbServiceConfig, ServerConfig, routers, HTTPS examples

- MentisDbServiceConfig: comprehensive type-level doc with field table and
  full embedding example (mcp_router → Axum serve); each field and every
  with_* builder method gets rich prose + runnable # Examples block

- MentisDbServerConfig: merged duplicate doc blocks; added full MENTISDB_*
  env-var reference table; each field documents its env-var override and
  semantics; from_env gets an exhaustive example including post-construction
  field overrides

- ServerHandle: rewritten with graceful-shutdown pattern example; new(),
  local_addr(), and shutdown() all have # Examples / # Errors sections

- MentisDbServerHandles: documents when each Option field is None; shows
  how to print every active address and shut all servers down gracefully

- mcp_router: documents legacy CloudLLM surface, when to prefer it over
  standard_mcp_router, and a nest() embedding example

- standard_mcp_router: documents the streamable-HTTP root endpoint,
  comparison table vs mcp_router, and a merge() embedding example

- rest_router: full endpoint inventory table, when-to-use guidance, and
  a custom-route merge example

- start_servers: documents the 6-step startup sequence (TLS cert, 5
  server variants, shared REST service arc)

- start_https_mcp_server / start_https_rest_server: document TLS cert
  requirements, rcgen self-signed workflow, and show concrete examples

- start_tls_router (private): documents rustls/axum-server wiring and
  the 5-second graceful-drain timeout

- ensure_tls_cert (private): documents cert properties in a table,
  auto-creation trigger, and override via MENTISDB_TLS_CERT/KEY

- default_mentisdb_dir: documents the 3-step priority chain
  (MENTISDB_DIR → /Users/gubatron/.cloudllm/mentisdb → ./.cloudllm/mentisdb)

- adopt_legacy_default_mentisdb_dir: documents when it is called,
  the 4-step migration logic, and return value semantics with example

- LegacyDefaultStorageMigration: each field describes fast-path vs
  merge-path semantics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(startup): live per-agent thought count in startup report

print_agent_registry_summary was printing AgentRecord.thought_count
(stale stored value) instead of counting thoughts live. Now filters
chain.thoughts() by agent_id at startup, same as the dashboard API.

refresh_registered_chain_counts only fixes chain-level totals in the
registry; it never touched AgentRecord.thought_count, which is why
agents like astro showed 922 memories against a 194-thought chain.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(dashboard): live thought_count in api_get_agent

The single-agent GET endpoint was still returning the stale stored
thought_count from AgentRecord. Now computes it live by filtering
chain.thoughts() by agent_id, consistent with api_agents_all and
api_agents_by_chain.

commit.message:
feat(api): enforce squad number immutability on PUT (#529)

Add a mismatch guard in put_async: if squad_number in the request
body does not match the path parameter, return HTTP 400 Bad Request.
The path parameter is the authoritative source of identity on PUT.

Document the single-model design decision in PlayerRequestModel:
one model intentionally covers both POST and PUT, with per-operation
differences handled at the route layer.

commit.message:
fix(dashboard): use 'timestamp' field for thought dates, 'last_seen_at' for agents

The thought API returns 'timestamp' not 'created_at'. The agent API returns
'first_seen_at'/'last_seen_at' not 'created_at'/'updated_at'.
All date columns were showing '—' due to missing field name mapping.

Fix: fmtDate(t.timestamp||t.created_at) with fallback for forward-compat.
Agent detail shows 'First seen' / 'Last seen' from correct field names.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add download-index command for pre-built index from GitHub Releases

Users can now get semantic search in ~30 seconds instead of 15-30 minutes:
  npx @jasonbellz/gospel-library-mcp download-index

Downloads index.db from the latest GitHub Release and saves it to
~/.gospel-library-mcp/index.db. Uses atomic write (.tmp + rename)
to avoid corruption if interrupted.

Also:
- Export DB_DIR from vectorStore.ts (needed by downloader)
- Update startup/search fallback messages to suggest download-index first
- Update build-index message to reflect title+description indexing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: gospel-library-mcp

commit.message:
skill: evolve MENTISDB_SKILL.md to v3 with 10 new best practices from chain traversal

Traversed borganism-brain genesis→head (192 thoughts) to identify undocumented
usage patterns discovered by agents in actual use.

New sections added:
- Session Start Patterns: Bootstrap Memory (first-ever join Summary) and
  Session Reload Summary (re-entry at session start) as distinct patterns
- TaskComplete as the definitive final act for leaf-task sub-agents vs Summary
- Domain Knowledge Dumps as Insight: specialists write snapshot of their domain
  knowledge so all future agents on the chain benefit
- Fleet Operating Protocols as Constraint Thoughts: makes protocols durable+queryable
- PM Mistake and Wonder Thoughts: PM self-critique and aspirational engineering
- REST API Direct Usage: curl examples for HTTP-based memory writes

Updated Fleet Anti-Patterns:
- Added: not pre-registering sub-agents
- Added: assigning parallel agents overlapping file ownership
- Added: closing a leaf task with only Summary instead of TaskComplete

Updated ThoughtType guidance:
- Wonder: aspirational engineering wishes not yet committed enough for Plan
- TaskComplete: concrete deliverable done vs Summary for state snapshot
- Mistake: wrong action taken (vs Correction for wrong fact)

1032 → 1196 lines. Skill v3 uploaded to registry (6191e6a1).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: web dashboard at /dashboard (port 9475)

Self-contained HTML dashboard embedded in the mentisdbd binary via
include_str! — no npm, no Node.js, zero new crate dependencies.

New files:
- src/dashboard.rs (791 lines): DashboardState, PIN auth middleware
  (Bearer header + mentisdb_pin cookie), Axum router under /dashboard,
  14 API handlers covering chains/thoughts/agents/skills
- src/dashboard_static/index.html (1287 lines): full SPA — hash-based
  router, 6 views, 28-type grouped ThoughtType multi-select filter,
  thought detail modal, agent metadata edit + key management, skill
  markdown viewer with Rendered/Source/Diff tabs, XSS-safe esc()
- src/dashboard_static/login.html (102 lines): PIN entry page

Dashboard API endpoints (all under /dashboard/api/):
  GET  /chains                                 — chain list with live counts
  GET  /chains/:key/thoughts                   — paginated, filterable by type
  GET  /thoughts/:key/:id                      — single thought detail
  GET  /chains/:key/agents/:aid/thoughts       — agent-scoped thought view
  GET  /agents                                 — all agents across chains
  GET  /agents/:key  /agents/:key/:id          — per-chain / single agent
  PATCH /agents/:key/:id                       — update display_name/description/owner
  POST /agents/:key/:id/revoke|activate        — status toggle
  POST /agents/:key/:id/keys                   — add ed25519 public key
  DELETE /agents/:key/:id/keys/:kid            — revoke key
  GET  /skills  /skills/:id  /skills/:id/versions  /skills/:id/diff

Modified:
- src/server.rs: MentisDbService.{chains,skills} pub(crate); added
  dashboard_addr/dashboard_pin to MentisDbServerConfig + from_env();
  dashboard field on MentisDbServerHandles; start_dashboard_server();
  shares Arc<DashMap> chains + Arc<RwLock<SkillRegistry>> with dashboard
- src/lib.rs: pub(crate) mod dashboard under server feature flag
- src/bin/mentisdbd.rs: dashboard URL in startup catalog,
  MENTISDB_DASHBOARD_PORT + MENTISDB_DASHBOARD_PIN in config block
- .env.example: Web Dashboard section

New env vars:
  MENTISDB_DASHBOARD_PORT  (default: 9475; 0 = disabled)
  MENTISDB_DASHBOARD_PIN   (default: unset = open access)

All 32 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add changelog

commit.message:
Add contextual Tips & Tricks section for remaining daily protein goal (#7)

Users had no in-app guidance on how to close the gap between their
current intake and daily protein goal. This adds a dynamic tips section
that surfaces food suggestions calibrated to the remaining amount.

### Changes

- **`index.html`** — New `#tips-section` container placed below Today's
Log; hidden by default via `display:none`
- **`app.js`**
  - `getTips(remaining)` — returns 3 contextual tips across 4 tiers:
    | Remaining | Suggestions |
    |-----------|-------------|
    | ≥ 60g | Chicken breast 200g, tuna + shake combo, meal planning |
    | 30–59g | Whey shake, tuna 100g, Greek yogurt + cottage cheese |
    | 10–29g | Greek yogurt 150g, two eggs, cottage cheese 100g |
    | < 10g | Milk 200ml, one egg, cottage cheese 50g |
- `renderTips()` — shows/hides section based on remaining; hidden once
goal is met
- `render()` now calls `renderTips()` so tips update live on every entry
change
- **`style.css`** — `.tip-item` uses a green left-border accent
(`border-left: 3px solid var(--green-500)`) and reuses existing
surface/text-muted variables; consistent with the log item design

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->



<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> erstelle einen Bereich mit Tipps und Tricks zur Erreichung des
verbliebenen Rest

repository.name: mentisdb
repository.description: Memory that lasts and compounds. MentisDB gives agents durable memory so they do not just remember, they improve over time. It stores append-only thought chains   plus a Git-like skills registry, letting skills evolve with experience through versioned, integrity-checked updates and fast retrieval.

commit.message:
fix: refresh stale chain registry counts on startup, fix off-by-one on append

- Move persist_registries() to after thoughts.push() in append_thought so
  the written thought_count always equals the post-append length (fixes the
  persistent off-by-one where registry stored N-1 instead of N).

- Add public refresh_registered_chain_counts() that opens each registered
  chain, reads live thought/agent counts, and rewrites the registry JSON only
  when at least one entry has drifted.  Only counts that changed are updated.

- Call refresh_registered_chain_counts() at daemon startup (mentisdbd.rs) after
  migrations, before server start, so any pre-existing stale counts inherited
  from older versions or crashes are repaired on first boot.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: mentisdb
repository.description: Memory that lasts and compounds. MentisDB gives agents durable memory so they do not just remember, they improve over time. It stores append-only thought chains   plus a Git-like skills registry, letting skills evolve with experience through versioned, integrity-checked updates and fast retrieval.

commit.message:
fix: chain summary shows live thought/agent counts instead of stale registry cache

The registry JSON only updates thought_count and agent_count during migration,
so those fields go stale as new thoughts accumulate. print_chain_summary() now
opens each chain file directly to read live counts, falling back to the cached
values only if the chain cannot be opened.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: MENTISDB_THOUGHT_SOUNDS — per-thought-type audio + jingle timing fix

- Fix jingle timing: play_startup_jingle() now fires after print_banner()
  + explicit stdout flush, so the banner is always visible before audio starts

- Add MENTISDB_THOUGHT_SOUNDS env var (default false):
  - When enabled, every successful thought append triggers a unique
    ≤200ms square-wave sound sequence in a spawn_blocking task
  - 28 ThoughtType variants each have a distinct melodic signature:
    - Surprise → rapid MGS-style ascending arpeggio (C5→E5→G5→C6, 35ms each)
    - Mistake   → high-to-low descent (deflating)
    - Correction → minor-to-major resolution rise
    - TaskComplete → C major arpeggio up
    - Insight / Idea → bright upward jumps
    - PatternDetected → palindromic pattern (C5→E5→C5)
    - Handoff / Summary → descending settling tones
    - ... and 21 more

- Refactor: extract play_notes() helper; play_startup_jingle() and
  play_thought_sound() both call it

- Add on_thought_appended: Option<Arc<dyn Fn(ThoughtType) + Send + Sync>>
  to MentisDbServiceConfig (with manual Debug impl); remove derive(Debug)
  replaced by impl fmt::Debug; add with_on_thought_appended() builder

- Hook callback in MentisDbService::append() and append_retrospective()
  via tokio::task::spawn_blocking

- Add MENTISDB_THOUGHT_SOUNDS to Configuration printout and .env.example

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
